ScopeItemStatement: The project shall produce a free and open-source piping stress analysis platform.
Notes: Open-source licensing choice remains TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The software shall remain code-neutral: the solver computes mechanics and user-supplied rule packs evaluate acceptability.
Notes: Do not encode a proprietary code as the default public product.

ScopeItemStatement: The public repository shall not redistribute protected standards text, tables, figures, examples, material allowables, SIF tables, flexibility-factor tables, or protected dimensional tables.
Notes: Legal review required before accepting any public data contribution.

ScopeItemStatement: The system shall require users to supply code-specific and project-specific data needed for code checks.
SourceRef: INTENT.md §User-supplied code and data layer; PRD §6.1, §12
Notes: Rule-pack completeness must be machine-checkable.

ScopeItemStatement: The analytical core shall model piping as a 3D centerline/frame system with six degrees of freedom per node.
Notes: Primary global model is line-element, not routine solid meshing.

ScopeItemStatement: The solver shall implement straight pipe elements with open mechanics section and stiffness calculations.
Notes: Pipe dimensions come from user data or lawful libraries.

ScopeItemStatement: The component model shall support bends and elbows with user-entered SIFs, flexibility factors, and bend geometry.
Notes: Do not bundle B31J-derived tables/equations.

ScopeItemStatement: The component model shall support branch connections with user-entered reinforcement, SIF, flexibility, and local data fields.
Notes: Branch local checks may require future specialized modules.

ScopeItemStatement: The component model shall support rigid and semi-rigid valves, flanges, reducers, and specialty items using user-supplied dimensions, weights, and centers of gravity.
Notes: Public component data must be licensed.

ScopeItemStatement: The component model shall support expansion joints using manufacturer/user-supplied stiffness, effective area, movement limits, and hardware data.
Notes: No invented expansion joint defaults.

ScopeItemStatement: The solver shall support anchors, guides, line stops, vertical supports, springs, and imposed displacements.
Notes: Initial release may limit to linear supports.

ScopeItemStatement: The solver shall support nonlinear support behavior such as one-way restraints, lift-off, gaps, and friction through a controlled iterative method.
Notes: MVP may defer some nonlinear cases.

ScopeItemStatement: The analysis engine shall support primitive load cases for weight, pressure, thermal expansion, imposed displacement, hydrotest, wind, seismic, and occasional loads.
Notes: Dynamic modules may be deferred.

ScopeItemStatement: The analysis engine shall support unit-aware load-case algebra and user-defined combinations.
Notes: Code-specific combinations supplied by user rule packs.

ScopeItemStatement: The stress module shall recover fundamental mechanics stresses from forces, moments, pressure, and section properties.
Notes: Code stress categories are rule-pack mappings.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall provide a private rule-pack schema for user-defined stress checks, allowables, formulas, and pass/fail criteria.
Notes: Public examples must use invented values.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall support private material libraries with temperature-dependent properties, allowables, and provenance fields.
Notes: No public ASME material allowable tables.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall support private pipe section and component libraries with provenance and redistribution status.
Notes: Public data requires documented rights.

ScopeItemStatement: The public project shall accept component data only when provenance and redistribution rights are documented.
Notes: Contributor certification required.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI shall provide a 3D centerline modeler with model tree and piping component visualization.
Notes: GUI framework TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI shall provide editors for materials, sections, components, load cases, supports, rule packs, and private libraries.
Notes: Workflow should surface missing data early.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI shall distinguish data required for solving from data required for code checking and shall block/qualify results when required data is missing.
Notes: No silent engineering defaults.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI shall provide results review for displacements, rotations, forces, moments, restraint reactions, equipment loads, stresses, and ratios.
Notes: Stress ratios depend on rule pack completeness.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall generate auditable calculation reports including inputs, sources, warnings, assumptions, results, rule-pack checksums, and limitations.
Notes: Reports must not reproduce protected standards content.

ScopeItemStatement: All calculations, schemas, imports, exports, and rule evaluations shall be unit-aware.
Notes: Unit conversions must be deterministic and testable.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall maintain verification benchmarks, regression tests, and numerical quality checks for solver and stress recovery behavior.
Notes: Benchmark sources must be public/original/permissive.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall maintain a validation manual that distinguishes mechanics verification from code compliance and professional signoff.
Notes: Human engineering judgment remains required.

ScopeItemStatement: The repository shall include contributor governance, IP controls, and review procedures preventing copyrighted standards data from entering the public project.
Notes: Use stop-and-escalate for suspected protected content.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall be local-first and shall protect private rule packs, private material data, private component data, and project models.
Notes: Cloud services are out of MVP unless approved.

ScopeItemStatement: The architecture shall support public APIs, plugins, and import/export adapters for integration with external tools.
Notes: Specific formats TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall support optional handoff of selected local-detail problems to external shell/solid FEA workflows.
Notes: Local FEA is not the normal global analysis method.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall provide reproducible build, packaging, and CI/CD workflows for supported platforms.
Notes: Supported OS/platforms TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall provide user/developer documentation and invented-data examples for education and testing.
Notes: No protected examples.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall preserve professional responsibility boundaries: agents/software do not certify, approve, seal, or declare engineering code compliance for reliance.
Notes: Reports may show user-rule pass/fail, not human code compliance.

ScopeItemStatement: The solver shall be designed for sparse numerical performance and reproducible results on practical piping models.
Notes: Performance targets TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI and reports shall support baseline accessibility and usability appropriate for engineering review.
Notes: Detailed WCAG target TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: Telemetry, if implemented, shall be opt-in, privacy-preserving, and shall not transmit private project/code data.
Notes: MVP default is no telemetry.

ScopeItemStatement: The software architecture shall be extensible without allowing plugins/adapters to bypass governance, unit safety, or data-boundary constraints.
Notes: Plugin trust model TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall produce reproducibility artifacts such as model hashes, solver version, rule-pack checksum, and input manifest.
Notes: Hash/canonicalization details TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall implement private data handling controls, including redaction/export safeguards where reports or shared models may expose protected/private values.
Notes: Detailed threat model TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall define machine-readable project, model, material, component, load, result, and report schemas.
Notes: Schema language TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: Rule packs shall be versioned, source-noted, checksum-addressed, and explicitly marked for redistribution status.
Notes: Private rule packs should not be committed publicly.

ScopeItemStatement: The report system shall prohibit automatic inclusion of protected code text, copied standards tables, or proprietary formulas in generated public reports/templates.
Notes: User-private report templates remain user responsibility.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall provide import mechanisms that record source/provenance/license metadata for component and material data.
Notes: Importer may reject/flag missing provenance.

ScopeItemStatement: The rule-pack evaluator shall be sandboxed and unit-aware, not arbitrary executable code.
Notes: Expression language TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall support result exports suitable for review, regression comparison, and downstream tooling.
Notes: Export formats TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall clearly distinguish mechanics solved, rule-pack checked, and professionally code-compliant/human-approved states.
Notes: Human approval is outside software authority.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall define open-source license, governance, release, and maintainer policies.
Notes: License selection TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall define criteria for when global beam analysis is sufficient and when local shell/solid analysis handoff is needed.
Notes: Criteria are guidance, not automatic certification.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall support create/open/save/versioned project persistence with deterministic round-trip serialization of models, units, loads, rule-pack references, and provenance metadata.
Notes: Project file/storage format remains TBD.

ScopeItemStatement: The product shall calculate pipe section and mass properties from user-entered dimensions and material data with unit checks.
Notes: No protected pipe dimensional tables are introduced.

ScopeItemStatement: The load engine shall support concentrated forces, concentrated moments, and distributed user loads in addition to core primitive piping load categories.
Notes: Code-specific load combinations remain user/rule-pack supplied.

ScopeItemStatement: The solver shall detect and report singular, ill-conditioned, and nonconverged analysis states with deterministic diagnostics.
Notes: Supports the R1 exit criterion that the solver detects singular models.

ScopeItemStatement: The project shall provide a headless command-line or structured I/O analysis runner for early solver verification, regression, and automation workflows.
Notes: Provides the R0/R1 path before full GUI maturity.

ScopeItemStatement: The GUI shall keep solve execution reviewable with background solve execution, progress, cancellation, and diagnostic presentation.
Notes: Keeps GUI responsiveness and diagnostic traceability visible to users.
